rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE JEWEL DENT REVOCABLE LIVING TRUST DATED 8/30/17
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Issues to be addressed at hearing: 1) Notice is insufficient. Form DE-120 is the mandatory notice of hearing for this type of petition (Probate Code Section 17203). 2) The ending balance of cash assets in accounts must be verified. Verification is made by original bank or brokerage statements or original bank letters signed by a bank officer with the authority to sign, showing the vesting of the account, the date, and the amount of the balance. The statements or letters must encompass, not precede, the ending date of the accounting period (LRSF 14.34.D). 3) The accounting as presented is incomplete, with undescribed disbursements to unknown payees, and disbursements described as being paid from a "non-trust account" which is not explained. For the court to approve the account, bank statements must be filed, the trustee must provide clarity on the transactions unexplained in the account, and trustee must explain from where the "non-trust account disbursements" were paid. 4) No proposed order has been received or lodged with the court (LRSF 14.7.A). Please have the CASE NUMBER and the CASE NAME ready for the clerk. Parties appearing must check in for the hearing using their FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear in person or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. Please review LRSF 14.1. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 160 225 4765 and password is 514879. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE GEORGE F. THOMAS AND FREDA M. THOMAS REVOCABLE TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Invalidate Trust Amendments On The Grounds Of Undue Influence, Lack Of Capacity And Fraud; For Confirmation To Omitted Of Share Of Deceased Spouse'S Property Etc.
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 01/20/2026. Issues necessitating a continuance: 1) A petition involving a trust must state the names and last known addresses of all vested or contingent beneficiaries (CRC 7.902). 2) Notice is insufficient. At least 30 days before the time set for the hearing on the petition, the petitiner shall cause notice of hearing to be delivered to all beneficiaries (Probate Code Section 17203(2)). 3) Please clarify whether petitioner is pursuing from one of the two petitions filed, or both. 4) No proposed order has been recieved or lodged with the court. Any party lodging a proposed order in a contested matter must file proof of service of the proposed order on all parties entitled to notice of the petition (LRSF 14.7.A.4). Please address the above issues and deliver courtesy copies of all further filings to this examiner no later than 01/06/2026 to avoid additional continuance.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF HAROLD F. POHL
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Hearing For Filing Of A Petition For Final Distribution Or A Petition For Approval Of Report On Status Of Administration `
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 1/13/2026. The Court needs to determine whether the estate of the deceased executor, Charles, has been probated or will be probated in the future. If probate proceedings for his estate have begun or will begin, the successor personal representative (PR) of Paul's estate must identify the legal representative of Charles's estate and provide proof, such as Letters of Administration. This information is essential for the Court to direct the fiduciary or the attorney of Charles to prepare the final account and report on Charles's behalf. If the rightful successors in interest (the legal representatives of Charles's estate) submit a written waiver of the accounting, and this waiver is filed two weeks prior to the next hearing, the Court may consider taking this status hearing off calendar and schedule a future hearing for the successor personal representative to file a petition for final distribution.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE SHERAK-CHAN FAMILY TRUST DATED JUNE 10, 2016 TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Confirming Trust Assets
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The judicial officer is to review and determine: 1) Is San Francisco the proper county for this trust petition? It should be noted that Probate Code section 7051 cited in the supplement applies to decedent estate cases (and not to trust cases). The examples of petitioner's activities physically carried on as trustee within San Francisco do not appear to be the "day-to-day" trustee activities within the meaning of section 17002(a) to support venue in San Francisco as the principal place of administration of the trust under section 17005(a)(1). Unless the San Francisco real property is the only asset of the trust, the principal place of administration of the trust appears to be Minnesota where petitioner resides (see section 17002(b)(1)) and where petitioner also presumably carries on the day-to-day activities of the trust (e.g., review financial documents, pay bills, etc.). In the absence of the principal place of administration of the trust in California (see section 17005(c) and Law Revision Commission Comments on section 17005), the proper venue appears to lie in Sonoma County under CCP section 392(a)(1) where the real property sought to be confirmed as an asset of the trust is located, but to the extent the petition prays for the validity of the trust as additional relief, should the Court accept venue in San Francisco by applying CCP section 395(a) allowing petitioner to designate any county? 2) The only real property listed on the schedule of trust assets was the settlors' home in San Francisco. The schedule of trust assets listed "all personal property" as assets intended to be transferred to the trust, but the schedule did not list "all real property". As Katharine Sherak acquired her TIC interest in the Sonoma property more than 4 years after creating the trust, the trust instrument predating Katharine's acquisition does not appear to qualify as her declaration "by the owner of the property that the owner holds (presently) the property as trustee" under section 15200(a). Katharine did not sign any written instrument (e.g., general assignment of property including real property) that appears to satisfy the statute of frauds required by section 15206 to transfer any real property interest to a trust. Katharine's pour-over will was presented as evidence of her intent to make an inter vivos transfer of her TIC interest in the Sonoma property, but section 13501(b) requires probate administration of "property disposed of in trust under the decedent's will" as a testamentary transfer to a trust contemplated by section 15200(c). PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.
